data.title: Is LeMaitre Vascular (LMAT) Pricing Too Rich After Mixed Long Term Returns?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether LeMaitre Vascular at around US$89.25 is priced fairly or not, this article will walk through what the current valuation actually implies for you as a shareholder or potential investor.
  • The stock shows a mix of recent returns, with gains of 1.4% over the last 7 days, 1.1% over 30 days and 11.3% year to date, but a 10.6% decline over 1 year, alongside longer term returns of 85.6% over 3 years and 98.2% over 5 years.
  • Recent news coverage around LeMaitre Vascular has focused on providing investors with evergreen context on the company, helping put its current share price and long term performance into perspective. That context is useful when you are trying to judge whether the recent return profile lines up with what you think the business is worth.
  • On our valuation checks, LeMaitre Vascular currently has a valuation score of 1 out of 6, which means it screens as undervalued on only one of the six measures we use. Next we will look at how different valuation approaches, from multiples to discounted cash flow, frame that score, and then finish with a way of looking at valuation that can tie the whole picture together for you.

LeMaitre Vascular scores just 1/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Approach 1: LeMaitre Vascular Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model takes forecasts of a company’s future cash flows and discounts them back to today using a required return rate, aiming to estimate what the business might be worth in today’s dollars.

For LeMaitre Vascular, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on cash flow projections. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about $65.56 million. Analysts provide free cash flow estimates out to 2030, with Simply Wall St extrapolating beyond the earlier analyst inputs. By 2030, projected free cash flow is $79.20 million, with a series of annual figures between 2026 and 2035 that are discounted back to today.

Pulling all of those discounted cash flows together gives an estimated intrinsic value of about $68.56 per share. Compared to the recent share price of around $89.25, the DCF output implies the stock is roughly 30.2% overvalued on this model.

Result: OVERVALUED

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for LeMaitre Vascular.

Approach 2: LeMaitre Vascular Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company like LeMaitre Vascular, the P/E ratio is a useful way to think about valuation because it links what you pay per share to the earnings the business is currently generating. Higher growth expectations and lower perceived risk usually justify a higher P/E, while slower growth and higher risk tend to support a lower, more conservative P/E as a "normal" range.

LeMaitre Vascular currently trades on a P/E of 37.96x. That sits above the Medical Equipment industry average P/E of 30.34x and also below the peer group average of 70.70x, so the stock is positioned between those two benchmarks.

Simply Wall St also offers a proprietary "Fair Ratio" of 19.78x. This is an estimate of what P/E might make sense for LeMaitre Vascular given factors like its earnings growth, risk profile, profit margins, industry and market cap. Because it is tailored to the company, the Fair Ratio can be more informative than a simple comparison with peers or the broad industry, which may have very different characteristics.

Comparing the current P/E of 37.96x with the Fair Ratio of 19.78x suggests the shares trade materially above that tailored benchmark.

Result: OVERVALUED

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation, so let us introduce you to Narratives, which are simply your story about a company tied directly to your assumptions for fair value, future revenue, earnings and margins, and then compared with today’s share price on Simply Wall St’s Community page where millions of investors share their views.

data.title: A Look At Savara (SVRA) Valuation After Recent FDA Progress And Strengthened Outlook
data.description:
Advertisement

Why Savara’s recent FDA progress is back in focus

Savara (SVRA) is back on investor radars after resubmitting its biologics license application, completing a tech transfer to Fujifilm, and aligning key analytical protocols with the FDA.

With an FDA filing decision expected in the near term and management expressing strong confidence in the updated package, the upcoming Guggenheim Securities Emerging Outlook, Biotech Summit presentation on February 11 is drawing extra attention from investors.

See our latest analysis for Savara.

At a share price of $5.73, Savara has seen a 22.7% 90 day share price return and a very strong 1 year total shareholder return of 104.6%. This suggests momentum has been building as investors weigh the resubmitted BLA, FDA interactions, and upcoming Guggenheim presentation.

If this regulatory story has your attention and you want to see what else is moving, now is a good time to scan 25 healthcare AI stocks for more potential ideas.

With the stock up strongly over the past year, trading at $5.73 and sitting well below the average analyst price target of $10.81, the key question is whether there is still a buying opportunity here or if the market is already pricing in future growth.

Preferred price to book of 15x, is it justified?

On a P/B of 15x, Savara trades well above the broader US Biotechs average of 2.6x, so the market is clearly paying up for this name.

P/B compares the company’s market value to its book value. It is often used for asset light, pre revenue or unprofitable biopharma businesses where traditional earnings metrics are less useful. For Savara, this high multiple sits alongside meaningful losses of $115.645m and no reported revenue, so the valuation appears to be anchored more to expectations than current financials.

Investors are assigning Savara a much richer valuation than the sector average. This suggests a focus on future revenue and earnings forecasts rather than today’s balance sheet. That premium stands in contrast to its low overall value score of 1, and leaves little room for this P/B ratio to look cheap relative to the wider US Biotechs group.

Latest FY 2025 Results Set the Stage for eToro Group (ETOR)

eToro Group (ETOR) has put fresh numbers on the board for FY 2025, with fourth quarter revenue of about US$3.9b and basic EPS of US$0.79, alongside full year trailing twelve month EPS of US$2.58 on revenue of roughly US$13.8b. The company has seen quarterly revenue move from US$5.8b in Q4 2024 to US$3.9b in Q4 2025, while basic EPS shifted from US$0.78 to US$0.79 over the same period. Trailing twelve month revenue has ranged between US$12.5b and US$15.7b, with EPS between US$2.58 and US$4.93. With net income remaining positive and margins inching higher over the past year, this result keeps attention firmly on how sustainable that profit profile really is.

See our full analysis for eToro Group.

With the headline figures on the table, the next step is to see how this mix of revenue scale, EPS progression, and margin stability lines up with the main narratives investors have been using to frame eToro Group's story.

Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

TTM Net Income Reaches US$215.7m

  • Over the last twelve months to Q4 2025, eToro Group recorded net income of US$215.7m on US$13.8b of revenue, compared with US$192.4m on US$12.5b of revenue at the end of Q4 2024.
  • What stands out for a bullish view is the mix of strong five year earnings growth of 41.9% per year and 12.1% earnings growth over the last year, while net margin sits at 1.6%. Supporters point to this track record even as they watch how that 1.6% margin behaves against the latest quarterly net income figures of US$68.7m in Q4 2025 and US$59.2m in Q4 2024.

Revenue Volatility Against 14.6% Earnings Forecast

  • Quarterly revenue has swung between US$1.5b and US$5.8b across the periods shown, and the supplied data points to earnings forecast growth of about 14.6% per year while also flagging a very large projected revenue decline of 136.3% per year over the next three years.
  • Bears focus on that projected revenue decline in the data and question how earnings can still be forecast to grow 14.6% per year, especially when trailing twelve month revenue is US$13.8b versus US$3.8b back in Q4 2023. They watch this tension between a growing profit base and the sharply negative revenue outlook as a key test of how durable the current 1.6% margin really is.

P/E Of 12.9x And 25.5% DCF Discount

  • At a share price of US$33.27, the stock trades on a P/E of 12.9x, below the US Capital Markets industry at 23.1x and peers at 20.4x, and below the stated DCF fair value of about US$44.64 by roughly 25.5%.

data.title: SOLXYZ's (TSE:4284) Performance Is Even Better Than Its Earnings Suggest
data.description:
Even though SOLXYZ Co., Ltd. (TSE:4284 ) posted strong earnings, investors appeared to be underwhelmed. We did some digging and actually think they are being unnecessarily pessimistic.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Advertisement

Examining Cashflow Against SOLXYZ's Earnings

As finance nerds would already know, the accrual ratio from cashflow is a key measure for assessing how well a company's free cash flow (FCF) matches its profit. The accrual ratio subtracts the FCF from the profit for a given period, and divides the result by the average operating assets of the company over that time. This ratio tells us how much of a company's profit is not backed by free cashflow.

As a result, a negative accrual ratio is a positive for the company, and a positive accrual ratio is a negative. That is not intended to imply we should worry about a positive accrual ratio, but it's worth noting where the accrual ratio is rather high. That's because some academic studies have suggested that high accruals ratios tend to lead to lower profit or less profit growth.

SOLXYZ has an accrual ratio of -0.14 for the year to December 2025. Therefore, its statutory earnings were very significantly less than its free cashflow. In fact, it had free cash flow of JP¥1.5b in the last year, which was a lot more than its statutory profit of JP¥839.0m. SOLXYZ shareholders are no doubt pleased that free cash flow improved over the last twelve months.

Note: we always recommend investors check balance sheet strength. Click here to be taken to our balance sheet analysis of SOLXYZ.

Our Take On SOLXYZ's Profit Performance

SOLXYZ's accrual ratio is solid, and indicates strong free cash flow, as we discussed, above. Because of this, we think SOLXYZ's earnings potential is at least as good as it seems, and maybe even better! And the EPS is up 63% annually, over the last three years.
